title: Learning a Generative Meta-Model of LLM Activations
description_en: Existing approaches for analyzing neural network activations, such as PCA and sparse autoencoders, rely on strong structural assumptions. Generative models offer an alternative: they can uncover structure without such assumptions and act as priors that improve intervention fidelity. We explore this direction by training diffusion models on one billion residual stream activations, creating "meta-models" that learn the distribution of a network's internal states. We find that diffusion loss decreases smoothly with compute and reliably predicts downstream utility. In particular, applying the meta-model's learned prior to steering interventions improves fluency, with larger gains as loss decreases. Moreover, the meta-model's neurons increasingly isolate concepts into individual units, with sparse probing scores that scale as loss decreases. These results suggest generative meta-models offer a scalable path toward interpretability without restrictive structural assumptions. Project page: https://generative-latent-prior.github.io.
description_zh: 该研究提出了一种全新的神经网络可解释性范式：不再依赖PCA或稀疏自编码器（SAE）等强结构假设，而是通过在十亿级残差流激活数据上训练扩散模型，构建“元模型（Meta-Model）”。该模型学习了网络内部状态的概率分布，充当先验知识。研究发现，扩散损失随计算量平滑下降，且能有效预测下游任务效用。在模型干预（Steering）中，该先验显著提升了生成文本的流畅度，且随着损失降低，神经元对概念的解耦能力增强。这为实现无约束、可扩展的AI可解释性提供了新的技术路径。
track_reason: 纯技术论文，探索新的算法和模型。

full_profile.one_liner: Learning a Generative Meta-Model of LLM Activations — 通过扩散模型学习LLM内部激活分布的元模型，实现无假设的神经网络可解释性，由UC Berke
full_profile.overview_zh: 该研究提出了一种全新的神经网络可解释性范式：不再依赖PCA或稀疏自编码器（SAE）等强结构假设，而是通过在十亿级残差流激活数据上训练扩散模型，构建“元模型（Meta-Model）”。该模型学习了网络内部状态的概率分布，充当先验知识。研究发现，扩散损失随计算量平滑下降，且能有效预测下游任务效用。在模型干预（Steering）中，该先验显著提升了生成文本的流畅度，且随着损失降低，神经元对概念的解耦能力增强。这为实现无约束、可扩展的AI可解释性提供了新的技术路径。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 该项目目前为纯学术研究，暂无商业化变现计划。若未来商业化，其潜在路径包括：1. 为大模型厂商提供模型审计与安全性评估工具（B2B SaaS）；2. 提供模型可解释性API，帮助开发者调试黑盒模型；3. 授权核心算法专利。由于其属于底层技术突破，商业价值取决于其能否成为AI安全与对齐领域的行业标准工具。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone视角：该方向具有极高的Alpha潜力，因为它直接挑战了当前可解释性研究（如SAE）的瓶颈。目前市场过度依赖SAE，但SAE存在严重的结构假设偏差。通过生成式模型学习激活分布，本质上是将可解释性问题转化为生成建模问题，这符合Scaling Laws的逻辑。风险在于计算成本极高，且如何将这种“元模型”转化为可落地的工程化工具尚不明确。我们的投资论文将关注该技术在模型对齐（Alignment）和鲁棒性增强方面的实际应用转化。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native得18分，因为其核心是利用生成式AI去理解AI，属于元学习范畴；Niche得21分，因为其避开了主流的SAE路径，具有极强的非共识性；商业模式得7分，因目前为纯学术项目，缺乏变现路径；团队得14分，作者阵容包含OpenAI和顶级学术机构背景，具备极高技术权威；加分项包括其在可解释性领域的范式转移潜力（+10）及顶级作者团队（+5）；减分项因缺乏商业数据（-3）。总分72分，属于值得深度关注的底层技术突破，但距离商业化仍有距离。
full_profile.metrics_summary: 暂无公开数据，仅有Arxiv论文发布及项目主页。

title: Inspector
description_en: Inspector is a visual editor that connects to your favorite AI agent (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor). Click on an element in your UI, tweak it visually, and Inspector writes the change to your codebase. No more design handoff, just push to the repo.
description_zh: Inspector 是一款旨在简化前端开发流程的 AI 辅助工具。它通过可视化编辑器连接现有的 AI Agent（如 Claude Code、Cursor 等），允许开发者直接在 UI 界面上进行点击和拖拽修改，Inspector 会自动将这些视觉调整转化为代码并推送到代码库中。该产品解决了传统开发中“设计稿到代码”的繁琐交付过程，目标用户是追求极致开发效率的前端工程师和独立开发者。目前已通过 Product Hunt 验证了初步的市场热度，展示了开发者对 Agent 辅助 UI 开发的强烈需求。
track_reason: 开发者工具，用于构建和修改AI Agent的代码。

score_breakdown.reason: 产品切入点精准但壁垒较低，处于开发者工具的竞争红海，69分反映了其市场热度与潜在的平台风险平衡。
plus: Product Hunt 502 票，社区认可度高
深度集成 Claude Code 等主流 Agent 生态
切中前端开发中设计交付的痛点

full_profile.one_liner: Inspector — 可视化AI Agent代码编辑器，通过UI点击直接修改代码库，旨在消除设计交付鸿沟，Product Hunt高人气项目。
full_profile.overview_zh: Inspector 是一款旨在简化前端开发流程的 AI 辅助工具。它通过可视化编辑器连接现有的 AI Agent（如 Claude Code、Cursor 等），允许开发者直接在 UI 界面上进行点击和拖拽修改，Inspector 会自动将这些视觉调整转化为代码并推送到代码库中。该产品解决了传统开发中“设计稿到代码”的繁琐交付过程，目标用户是追求极致开发效率的前端工程师和独立开发者。目前已通过 Product Hunt 验证了初步的市场热度，展示了开发者对 Agent 辅助 UI 开发的强烈需求。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 目前主要采用订阅制或 API 接入付费模式。其定价逻辑基于开发者节省的时间成本和工作流的无缝集成。用户愿意付费是因为它直接减少了手动编写 CSS 或调整布局的重复性劳动。与竞品相比，Inspector 强调的是“可视化交互”而非单纯的“代码生成”，这种交互层面的差异化是其商业化的核心支撑点。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone 认为，Agent 辅助开发已从纯代码生成转向“多模态交互式编程”。Inspector 的 Alpha 信号在于它切中了 UI 开发中“所见即所得”与“代码版本控制”之间的断层。市场目前低估了 Agent 在前端交互层面的深度集成价值。风险在于，随着 Cursor 等 IDE 原生功能的迭代，这种第三方插件的生存空间可能被挤压。我们的投资论文倾向于寻找能够成为开发者工作流“粘合剂”的工具，Inspector 具备成为此类基础设施的潜力，但需观察其能否在生态中建立长期的技术壁垒。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native 得 20 分，因为它实现了 UI 到代码的闭环，但目前仍依赖外部 Agent，自进化能力有限。Niche 得 14 分，UI 交互式编程是刚需，但技术实现难度相对较低，容易被 IDE 厂商集成。商业模式得 13 分，逻辑清晰但规模化尚需验证。团队得 10 分，背景信息不足。加分项包括 Product Hunt 502 票的高人气证明了社区认可（+2）以及对 Claude Code 生态的深度适配（+3），额外加 2 分社区热度。减分项主要是创始人背景信息缺失（-5），且作为第三方插件存在被平台方降维打击的风险。
full_profile.metrics_summary: Product Hunt: 502 票；GitHub stars: 暂无公开数据；用户数: 暂无公开数据；ARR: 暂无公开数据。

description_zh: Monty是一个由Pydantic核心团队开发的极简Python解释器，完全使用Rust编写。其核心目标是为AI Agent提供一个比标准CPython更安全、更轻量、更易于控制的执行环境。在AI Agent自主编写并执行代码的场景中，安全性（防止恶意代码逃逸）和可控性（资源限制、环境隔离）是关键瓶颈。Monty通过Rust的内存安全特性和极简的解释器架构，旨在成为Agent执行代码的底层基础设施，从而替代目前臃肿且难以安全加固的传统Python环境。

full_profile.one_liner: Monty — 由Pydantic团队开发的轻量级、安全Rust Python解释器，专为AI Agent环境设计，旨在解决LLM执行代码时的沙箱与安全性痛点。
full_profile.overview_zh: Monty是一个由Pydantic核心团队开发的极简Python解释器，完全使用Rust编写。其核心目标是为AI Agent提供一个比标准CPython更安全、更轻量、更易于控制的执行环境。在AI Agent自主编写并执行代码的场景中，安全性（防止恶意代码逃逸）和可控性（资源限制、环境隔离）是关键瓶颈。Monty通过Rust的内存安全特性和极简的解释器架构，旨在成为Agent执行代码的底层基础设施，从而替代目前臃肿且难以安全加固的传统Python环境。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 目前处于开源早期阶段，暂无明确商业化路径。作为Pydantic生态的一部分，其潜在商业模式可能包括：1. 为企业级Agent平台提供高性能、高安全性的商业版运行时；2. 通过与Pydantic Logfire等现有监控工具集成，构建闭环的Agent执行监控服务。目前主要价值在于通过开源建立行业标准，巩固Pydantic在AI数据处理领域的统治地位。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone视角：Monty切中了Agent基础设施中极具痛点的‘代码执行安全’领域。目前大多数Agent依赖Docker容器或复杂的沙箱来运行代码，开销大且延迟高。Monty利用Rust重写解释器，不仅能提供更细粒度的执行控制，还能显著降低Agent的响应延迟。Alpha在于其作为Pydantic生态的底层组件，极易在开发者群体中快速渗透。风险在于Python语言本身的复杂性，实现一个完全兼容且高性能的解释器难度极大，且需面对CPython生态兼容性的长期挑战。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native得24分，因为其直接针对Agent执行环境进行重构，属于底层基础设施创新；Niche得18分，因为代码执行安全是Agent落地的核心瓶颈，具有明确的技术护城河；商业模式得7分，因为目前仅为开源项目，变现路径尚不清晰；团队得12分，背靠Pydantic团队，具备极强的技术背书和开发者影响力；加分项得4分，主要得益于其在GitHub上极高的关注度（单日增长1300+ stars）以及Pydantic团队的品牌效应。未给更高分是因为项目尚处于极早期，实际生产环境的兼容性和稳定性尚未经过大规模验证。
full_profile.metrics_summary: GitHub stars今日增量: 1301; Forks: 120; 处于极早期阶段，暂无ARR或用户数数据。

title: Axel
description_en: Axel helps you run AI agents and keep them fed. Queue up work, dispatch to the right agent, and approve or deny actions from one inbox. It's native macOS, keyboard-driven, and works with Claude, Codex, OpenCode, and Antigravity out of the box. We hope it helps you ship faster 🚀
description_zh: Axel 是一款定位于 macOS 端的 Agent 调度与管理工具。它旨在解决开发者在多 Agent 环境下任务分发、执行监控及人工确认的碎片化问题。核心功能包括：一个统一的“收件箱”用于处理 Agent 的行动请求（批准或拒绝）、键盘驱动的交互界面，以及对 Claude、Codex 等主流模型的开箱即用支持。该产品将 Agent 的执行流从浏览器或终端中剥离出来，转化为本地化的桌面工作流，试图成为开发者日常开发中的 Agent 任务控制中心。
track_reason: 提供运行AI Agent的基础设施，方便开发者使用。

score_breakdown.reason: 项目处于早期阶段，UI/UX 优秀但缺乏核心技术壁垒，属于典型的工具型产品而非平台型机会。

full_profile.one_liner: Axel — macOS原生AI Agent调度与交互中心，通过统一收件箱管理多Agent任务流，由独立开发者构建。
full_profile.overview_zh: Axel 是一款定位于 macOS 端的 Agent 调度与管理工具。它旨在解决开发者在多 Agent 环境下任务分发、执行监控及人工确认的碎片化问题。核心功能包括：一个统一的“收件箱”用于处理 Agent 的行动请求（批准或拒绝）、键盘驱动的交互界面，以及对 Claude、Codex 等主流模型的开箱即用支持。该产品将 Agent 的执行流从浏览器或终端中剥离出来，转化为本地化的桌面工作流，试图成为开发者日常开发中的 Agent 任务控制中心。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 目前商业模式尚处于早期阶段，主要依赖于工具类产品的订阅制或买断制预期。由于其定位是提升开发者效率的生产力工具，定价逻辑通常基于个人生产力提升的溢价。用户愿意付费的原因在于其减少了在多个 Agent 网页端切换的上下文损耗。相比于 Dify 等平台级产品，Axel 的商业模式更偏向于轻量级桌面应用，竞争壁垒在于用户的使用习惯和交互体验。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone 视角：Agent 交互的“桌面化”是一个值得关注的趋势，随着 Agent 数量增加，用户需要一个统一的控制台（Control Plane）。Axel 的 Alpha 信号在于其对 macOS 原生体验的追求，这在目前充斥着 Web 端的 Agent 框架中具有差异化。然而，该方向面临极大的风险：一是操作系统厂商（如 Apple）可能在系统层面集成类似功能；二是通用 Agent 框架（如 LangChain 或 Cursor）可能会通过插件化吞噬此类工具的生存空间。目前该项目缺乏深度的技术护城河，更像是一个高效率的 UI/UX 封装。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native 得 18 分，因为它实现了 Agent 交互的本地化闭环，但缺乏自进化能力；Niche 得 12 分，因为其定位明确但技术壁垒较低，容易被 IDE 插件替代；商业模式得 10 分，目前尚无明确的 B2B 变现路径；团队得 9 分，背景信息有限，属于独立开发者项目；加分项在于其在 Product Hunt 上获得了 259 票的社区认可，且 macOS 原生体验有一定加分；减分项在于缺乏深度的技术壁垒，且作为独立工具面临大厂生态挤压的风险。综合来看，该项目处于 60-70 分区间，属于普通关注范畴。
full_profile.metrics_summary: Product Hunt: 259 票，GitHub stars/ARR 等暂无公开数据。

title: KeygraphHQ/shannon
description_en: Fully autonomous AI hacker to find actual exploits in your web apps. Shannon has achieved a 96.15% success rate on the hint-free, source-aware XBOW Benchmark.
description_zh: Shannon是一个旨在实现全自动Web应用渗透测试的AI Agent。它通过深度分析源代码，自主发现并验证安全漏洞，而非仅仅进行简单的扫描。其核心机制在于将复杂的渗透测试工作流（如侦察、漏洞利用、验证）转化为AI可执行的确定性任务。在无提示（hint-free）的XBOW基准测试中，Shannon达到了96.15%的成功率，证明了其在自动化安全评估领域的极高潜力。该产品主要面向安全研究人员、DevSecOps团队以及需要持续进行安全审计的企业，旨在解决传统渗透测试成本高、周期长且难以覆盖所有代码路径的问题。
track_reason: 开发者工具，用于构建AI Agent进行渗透测试。

full_profile.overview_zh: Shannon是一个旨在实现全自动Web应用渗透测试的AI Agent。它通过深度分析源代码，自主发现并验证安全漏洞，而非仅仅进行简单的扫描。其核心机制在于将复杂的渗透测试工作流（如侦察、漏洞利用、验证）转化为AI可执行的确定性任务。在无提示（hint-free）的XBOW基准测试中，Shannon达到了96.15%的成功率，证明了其在自动化安全评估领域的极高潜力。该产品主要面向安全研究人员、DevSecOps团队以及需要持续进行安全审计的企业，旨在解决传统渗透测试成本高、周期长且难以覆盖所有代码路径的问题。

title: AIRS-Bench: a Suite of Tasks for Frontier AI Research Science Agents
description_en: LLM agents hold significant promise for advancing scientific research. To accelerate this progress, we introduce AIRS-Bench (the AI Research Science Benchmark), a suite of 20 tasks sourced from state-of-the-art machine learning papers. These tasks span diverse domains, including language modeling, mathematics, bioinformatics, and time series forecasting. AIRS-Bench tasks assess agentic capabilities over the full research lifecycle -- including idea generation, experiment analysis and iterative refinement -- without providing baseline code. The AIRS-Bench task format is versatile, enabling easy integration of new tasks and rigorous comparison across different agentic frameworks. We establish baselines using frontier models paired with both sequential and parallel scaffolds. Our results show that agents exceed human SOTA in four tasks but fail to match it in sixteen others. Even when agents surpass human benchmarks, they do not reach the theoretical performance ceiling for the underlying tasks. These findings indicate that AIRS-Bench is far from saturated and offers substantial room for improvement. We open-source the AIRS-Bench task definitions and evaluation code to catalyze further development in autonomous scientific research.
description_zh: AIRS-Bench 是一个专门为科学研究 Agent 设计的评估基准，旨在解决当前 Agent 在复杂科研任务中缺乏标准化评估的问题。该套件包含 20 项源自顶尖机器学习论文的任务，覆盖语言模型、数学、生物信息学及时间序列预测等领域。其核心机制在于模拟完整的科研生命周期，包括从想法生成、实验设计到分析与迭代优化的全过程，且不提供基准代码，强制要求 Agent 具备自主推理与执行能力。研究表明，尽管 Agent 在部分任务上超越了人类 SOTA，但在大多数任务中仍有巨大差距，证明了该基准在推动科研自动化领域的长期价值。

title: Uncovering Cross-Objective Interference in Multi-Objective Alignment
description_en: We study a persistent failure mode in multi-objective alignment for large language models (LLMs): training improves performance on only a subset of objectives while causing others to degrade. We formalize this phenomenon as cross-objective interference and conduct the first systematic study across classic scalarization algorithms, showing that interference is pervasive and exhibits strong model dependence.   To explain this phenomenon, we derive a local covariance law showing that an objective improves at first order when its reward exhibits positive covariance with the scalarized score. We extend this analysis to clipped surrogate objectives used in modern alignment, demonstrating that the covariance law remains valid under mild conditions despite clipping. Building on this analysis, we propose Covariance Targeted Weight Adaptation (CTWA), a plug-and-play method that maintains positive covariance between objective rewards and the training signal to effectively mitigate cross-objective interference. Finally, we complement these local improvement conditions with a global convergence analysis under the Polyak--Łojasiewicz condition, establishing when non-convex scalarized optimization achieves global convergence and how cross-objective interference depends on specific model geometric properties.
description_zh: 该研究聚焦于大模型多目标对齐（Multi-Objective Alignment）中的核心痛点：在优化多个目标（如有用性、安全性、简洁性）时，往往会出现“顾此失彼”的现象，即部分目标提升的同时导致其他目标性能下降。作者将此现象形式化为“跨目标干扰”（Cross-Objective Interference），并证明了其在现有标量化算法中的普遍性。核心贡献在于推导出了局部协方差定律（Local Covariance Law），解释了为何某些目标会退化，并据此提出了CTWA（Covariance Targeted Weight Adaptation）算法。CTWA作为一种即插即用的方法，通过维持奖励与训练信号之间的正协方差，有效缓解了干扰问题，并提供了全局收敛性分析。

title: RFDM: Residual Flow Diffusion Model for Efficient Causal Video Editing
description_en: Instructional video editing applies edits to an input video using only text prompts, enabling intuitive natural-language control. Despite rapid progress, most methods still require fixed-length inputs and substantial compute. Meanwhile, autoregressive video generation enables efficient variable-length synthesis, yet remains under-explored for video editing. We introduce a causal, efficient video editing model that edits variable-length videos frame by frame. For efficiency, we start from a 2D image-to-image (I2I) diffusion model and adapt it to video-to-video (V2V) editing by conditioning the edit at time step t on the model's prediction at t-1. To leverage videos' temporal redundancy, we propose a new I2I diffusion forward process formulation that encourages the model to predict the residual between the target output and the previous prediction. We call this Residual Flow Diffusion Model (RFDM), which focuses the denoising process on changes between consecutive frames. Moreover, we propose a new benchmark that better ranks state-of-the-art methods for editing tasks. Trained on paired video data for global/local style transfer and object removal, RFDM surpasses I2I-based methods and competes with fully spatiotemporal (3D) V2V models, while matching the compute of image models and scaling independently of input video length. More content can be found in: https://smsd75.github.io/RFDM_page/
description_zh: RFDM (Residual Flow Diffusion Model) 旨在解决当前视频编辑模型对固定长度输入依赖及高计算成本的问题。该模型通过将2D图像到图像（I2I）扩散模型适配为视频到视频（V2V）编辑，引入了因果推理机制，即当前帧的编辑条件依赖于上一帧的预测结果。核心创新在于提出了一种新的扩散前向过程，鼓励模型预测目标输出与前一帧预测之间的“残差”，从而利用视频的时间冗余性，将去噪过程聚焦于帧间变化。该方法在全局/局部风格迁移和对象移除任务中表现优于传统I2I方法，并提出了新的基准测试以评估编辑效果。

title: Parameters as Experts: Adapting Vision Models with Dynamic Parameter Routing
description_en: Adapting pre-trained vision models using parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) remains challenging, as it aims to achieve performance comparable to full fine-tuning using a minimal number of trainable parameters. When applied to complex dense prediction tasks, existing methods exhibit limitations, including input-agnostic modeling and redundant cross-layer representations. To this end, we propose AdaRoute, a new adapter-style method featuring a simple mixture-of-experts (MoE) architecture. Specifically, we introduce shared expert centers, where each expert is a trainable parameter matrix. During a feedforward pass, each AdaRoute module in the network dynamically generates weight matrices tailored for the current module via a simple dynamic parameter routing mechanism, which selectively aggregates parameter matrices in the corresponding expert center. Dynamic weight matrices in AdaRoute modules facilitate low-rank adaptation in an input-dependent manner, thus generating more customized and powerful feature representations. Moreover, since AdaRoute modules across multiple network layers share the same expert center, they improve feature diversity by promoting implicit cross-layer feature interaction. Extensive experiments demonstrate the superiority of AdaRoute on diverse vision tasks, including semantic segmentation, object detection and instance segmentation, and panoptic segmentation. Code will be available at: https://bit.ly/3NZcr0H.
description_zh: AdaRoute 旨在解决预训练视觉模型在复杂密集预测任务中微调效率与性能的权衡问题。现有PEFT方法（如LoRA）通常是输入无关的，且存在跨层冗余。AdaRoute 引入了共享专家中心（Shared Expert Centers）架构，每个AdaRoute模块根据输入动态路由并聚合专家参数，生成定制化的权重矩阵。这种机制实现了输入依赖的低秩自适应，增强了特征表示的灵活性。通过在多层间共享专家中心，该方法不仅减少了参数量，还通过隐式跨层特征交互提升了模型在复杂视觉任务上的表现。

title: Rethinking Multi-Condition DiTs: Eliminating Redundant Attention via Position-Alignment and Keyword-Scoping
description_en: While modern text-to-image models excel at prompt-based generation, they often lack the fine-grained control necessary for specific user requirements like spatial layouts or subject appearances. Multi-condition control addresses this, yet its integration into Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) is bottlenecked by the conventional ``concatenate-and-attend'' strategy, which suffers from quadratic computational and memory overhead as the number of conditions scales. Our analysis reveals that much of this cross-modal interaction is spatially or semantically redundant. To this end, we propose Position-aligned and Keyword-scoped Attention (PKA), a highly efficient framework designed to eliminate these redundancies. Specifically, Position-Aligned Attention (PAA) linearizes spatial control by enforcing localized patch alignment, while Keyword-Scoped Attention (KSA) prunes irrelevant subject-driven interactions via semantic-aware masking. To facilitate efficient learning, we further introduce a Conditional Sensitivity-Aware Sampling (CSAS) strategy that reweights the training objective towards critical denoising phases, drastically accelerating convergence and enhancing conditional fidelity. Empirically, PKA delivers a 10.0$\times$ inference speedup and a 5.1$\times$ VRAM saving, providing a scalable and resource-friendly solution for high-fidelity multi-conditioned generation.
description_zh: 该研究针对当前多条件文本生成模型（如DiT）在处理空间布局和主体外观控制时存在的计算瓶颈问题，提出了PKA（Position-aligned and Keyword-scoped Attention）框架。传统模型采用“拼接并注意力”策略，导致计算量随条件增加呈二次方增长。PKA通过两项核心技术解决此问题：一是Position-Aligned Attention (PAA)，通过局部补丁对齐实现空间控制的线性化；二是Keyword-Scoped Attention (KSA)，利用语义感知掩码剪枝无关的跨模态交互。此外，引入的Conditional Sensitivity-Aware Sampling (CSAS)策略通过重加权去噪目标，显著提升了收敛速度和条件保真度。

title: Endogenous Resistance to Activation Steering in Language Models
description_en: Large language models can resist task-misaligned activation steering during inference, sometimes recovering mid-generation to produce improved responses even when steering remains active. We term this Endogenous Steering Resistance (ESR). Using sparse autoencoder (SAE) latents to steer model activations, we find that Llama-3.3-70B shows substantial ESR, while smaller models from the Llama-3 and Gemma-2 families exhibit the phenomenon less frequently. We identify 26 SAE latents that activate differentially during off-topic content and are causally linked to ESR in Llama-3.3-70B. Zero-ablating these latents reduces the multi-attempt rate by 25%, providing causal evidence for dedicated internal consistency-checking circuits. We demonstrate that ESR can be deliberately enhanced through both prompting and training: meta-prompts instructing the model to self-monitor increase the multi-attempt rate by 4x for Llama-3.3-70B, and fine-tuning on self-correction examples successfully induces ESR-like behavior in smaller models. These findings have dual implications: ESR could protect against adversarial manipulation but might also interfere with beneficial safety interventions that rely on activation steering. Understanding and controlling these resistance mechanisms is important for developing transparent and controllable AI systems. Code is available at github.com/agencyenterprise/endogenous-steering-resistance.
description_zh: 该研究发现大模型（如Llama-3.3-70B）在推理过程中具备一种“内源性转向抵抗”（ESR）能力，即在受到外部激活干预（Activation Steering）时，模型能自动识别并恢复至原始任务目标。研究通过稀疏自编码器（SAE）定位了26个与一致性检查相关的潜在特征（latents），并证明消融这些特征可显著降低模型纠偏能力。此外，研究展示了通过元提示（meta-prompting）和微调可增强这种内源性抵抗，这为模型鲁棒性与安全性提供了新的研究范式。

title: Robustness Beyond Known Groups with Low-rank Adaptation
description_en: Deep learning models trained to optimize average accuracy often exhibit systematic failures on particular subpopulations. In real world settings, the subpopulations most affected by such disparities are frequently unlabeled or unknown, thereby motivating the development of methods that are performant on sensitive subgroups without being pre-specified. However, existing group-robust methods typically assume prior knowledge of relevant subgroups, using group annotations for training or model selection. We propose Low-rank Error Informed Adaptation (LEIA), a simple two-stage method that improves group robustness by identifying a low-dimensional subspace in the representation space where model errors concentrate. LEIA restricts adaptation to this error-informed subspace via a low-rank adjustment to the classifier logits, directly targeting latent failure modes without modifying the backbone or requiring group labels. Using five real-world datasets, we analyze group robustness under three settings: (1) truly no knowledge of subgroup relevance, (2) partial knowledge of subgroup relevance, and (3) full knowledge of subgroup relevance. Across all settings, LEIA consistently improves worst-group performance while remaining fast, parameter-efficient, and robust to hyperparameter choice.
description_zh: LEIA (Low-rank Error Informed Adaptation) 旨在解决深度学习模型在处理未知子群（subpopulations）时表现出的系统性偏差问题。传统鲁棒性方法通常依赖于预先标注的子群信息，而LEIA通过两阶段法：首先在表示空间中识别误差集中的低维子空间，随后通过对分类器Logits进行低秩调整，直接针对潜在的故障模式进行修正。该方法无需修改骨干网络，也不需要任何子群标签，在多种真实世界数据集上验证了其在提升最差组表现（worst-group performance）方面的有效性。

title: From Kepler to Newton: Inductive Biases Guide Learned World Models in Transformers
description_en: Can general-purpose AI architectures go beyond prediction to discover the physical laws governing the universe? True intelligence relies on "world models" -- causal abstractions that allow an agent to not only predict future states but understand the underlying governing dynamics. While previous "AI Physicist" approaches have successfully recovered such laws, they typically rely on strong, domain-specific priors that effectively "bake in" the physics. Conversely, Vafa et al. recently showed that generic Transformers fail to acquire these world models, achieving high predictive accuracy without capturing the underlying physical laws. We bridge this gap by systematically introducing three minimal inductive biases. We show that ensuring spatial smoothness (by formulating prediction as continuous regression) and stability (by training with noisy contexts to mitigate error accumulation) enables generic Transformers to surpass prior failures and learn a coherent Keplerian world model, successfully fitting ellipses to planetary trajectories. However, true physical insight requires a third bias: temporal locality. By restricting the attention window to the immediate past -- imposing the simple assumption that future states depend only on the local state rather than a complex history -- we force the model to abandon curve-fitting and discover Newtonian force representations. Our results demonstrate that simple architectural choices determine whether an AI becomes a curve-fitter or a physicist, marking a critical step toward automated scientific discovery.
description_zh: 该研究探讨了通用Transformer架构在缺乏领域先验的情况下，是否能从数据中自动发现物理定律。研究指出，单纯的Transformer虽然预测准确率高，但往往无法捕捉底层的动力学规律。作者通过引入三个最小化的归纳偏置（空间平滑性、稳定性、时间局部性），成功使Transformer在开普勒行星运动轨迹预测任务中，不仅实现了高精度预测，还习得了符合物理规律的潜在世界模型。这为AI从“预测者”向“发现者”的范式转变提供了重要的理论支撑。

full_profile.biz_model_zh: 作为纯学术研究，本项目目前无商业模式。其价值在于为下一代AI架构设计提供理论基石，潜在的商业路径包括将这些归纳偏置集成到工业级仿真软件、科学计算AI平台或自动驾驶决策系统中，通过提升模型的可解释性与物理一致性来获取高溢价。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone视角：该研究触及了AI领域的核心痛点——如何让模型具备真正的物理世界理解力，而非仅仅是统计相关性的拟合。在科学发现（AI for Science）领域，这种从数据中提取因果律的能力具有极高的Alpha。市场目前过度关注Scaling Law，而低估了归纳偏置在小样本、高精度科学计算中的决定性作用。风险在于从实验室环境到复杂现实世界的泛化能力仍需验证，但该团队在AI与物理交叉领域的深厚背景，使其成为该赛道极具潜力的技术源头。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native得12分，因其属于底层架构优化而非应用层Agent；Niche得20分，因为将物理归纳偏置引入Transformer是极具前瞻性的学术突破；商业模式得5分，因其为纯学术论文，暂无变现路径；团队得15分，Ziming Liu与Surya Ganguli在AI理论界具有极高影响力；加分10分，因其解决了Transformer在科学计算中的关键瓶颈，且具备极高的学术影响力潜力。总分62分，反映了其作为顶级学术突破的价值，但距离商业化落地仍有较长距离。

title: Supercharging Simulation-Based Inference for Bayesian Optimal Experimental Design
description_en: Bayesian optimal experimental design (BOED) seeks to maximize the expected information gain (EIG) of experiments. This requires a likelihood estimate, which in many settings is intractable. Simulation-based inference (SBI) provides powerful tools for this regime. However, existing work explicitly connecting SBI and BOED is restricted to a single contrastive EIG bound. We show that the EIG admits multiple formulations which can directly leverage modern SBI density estimators, encompassing neural posterior, likelihood, and ratio estimation. Building on this perspective, we define a novel EIG estimator using neural likelihood estimation. Further, we identify optimization as a key bottleneck of gradient based EIG maximization and show that a simple multi-start parallel gradient ascent procedure can substantially improve reliability and performance. With these innovations, our SBI-based BOED methods are able to match or outperform by up to $22\%$ existing state-of-the-art approaches across standard BOED benchmarks.
description_zh: 该研究针对贝叶斯最优实验设计（BOED）中期望信息增益（EIG）计算困难的问题，提出了一种基于模拟推断（SBI）的创新框架。核心贡献在于：1. 证明了EIG可以通过多种SBI密度估计器（如神经后验、似然和比率估计）进行重构；2. 定义了一种基于神经似然估计（NLE）的新型EIG估计器；3. 引入了多起点并行梯度上升法，解决了梯度优化中的局部最优瓶颈。该方法在标准BOED基准测试中表现优于现有SOTA方案达22%，为物理、生物等需要复杂模拟的科学领域提供了更高效的实验设计工具。

title: Improved Sampling Schedules for Discrete Diffusion Models
description_en: Discrete diffusion models have emerged as a powerful paradigm for generative modeling on sequence data; however, the information-theoretic principles governing their reverse processes remain significantly less understood than those of their continuous counterparts. In this work, we bridge this gap by analyzing the reverse process dynamics through the lens of thermodynamic entropy production. We propose the entropy production rate as a rigorous proxy for quantifying information generation, deriving as a byproduct a bound on the Wasserstein distance between intermediate states and the data distribution. Leveraging these insights, we introduce two novel sampling schedules that are uniformly spaced with respect to their corresponding physics-inspired metrics: the Entropic Discrete Schedule (EDS), which is defined by maintaining a constant rate of information gain, and the Wasserstein Discrete Schedule (WDS), which is defined by taking equal steps in terms of the Wasserstein distance. We empirically demonstrate that our proposed schedules significantly outperform state-of-the-art strategies across diverse application domains, including synthetic data, music notation, vision and language modeling, consistently achieving superior performance at a lower computational budget.
description_zh: 该研究针对离散扩散模型（Discrete Diffusion Models）在序列数据生成中采样效率低、理论支撑薄弱的问题，引入了热力学熵产生率（Entropy Production Rate）作为量化信息生成的代理指标。通过该指标，研究者推导出了中间状态与数据分布之间Wasserstein距离的界限，并据此提出了两种新型采样调度算法：Entropic Discrete Schedule (EDS) 和 Wasserstein Discrete Schedule (WDS)。这些算法通过在物理度量空间内实现均匀步长，显著提升了模型在合成数据、音乐符号、视觉及语言任务中的生成性能，为离散扩散模型的采样优化提供了严谨的数学框架。

full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone 视角：Snap 处于 AI 开发者工具的边缘地带。虽然它试图通过优化交互来提升效率，但其提供的功能（如截图、语音转文字）缺乏深度的技术壁垒，极易被 Cursor 或 Claude Code 原生功能覆盖。市场低估了 IDE 原生插件的整合能力，而高估了独立浮动工具的价值。风险在于产品生命周期极短，一旦主流 IDE 厂商（如 Cursor）推出类似的原生功能，Snap 的生存空间将被瞬间压缩。我们的投资论文倾向于寻找具有数据飞轮或深度工作流整合能力的 Agent 基础设施，Snap 目前仅停留在 UI 交互层面。

title: MedMO: Grounding and Understanding Multimodal Large Language Model for Medical Images
description_en: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have rapidly advanced, yet their adoption in medicine remains limited by gaps in domain coverage, modality alignment, and grounded reasoning. In this work, we introduce MedMO, a medical foundation model built upon a generalized MLLM architecture and trained exclusively on large-scale, domain-specific data. MedMO follows a multi-stage training recipe: (i) cross-modal pretraining to align heterogeneous visual encoders with a medical language backbone; (ii) instruction tuning on multi-task supervision that spans captioning, VQA, report generation, retrieval, and grounded disease localization with bounding boxes; and (iii) reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards that combine factuality checks with a box-level GIoU reward to strengthen spatial grounding and step-by-step reasoning in complex clinical scenarios. MedMO consistently outperforms strong open-source medical MLLMs across multiple modalities and tasks. On VQA benchmarks, MedMO achieves an average accuracy improvement of +13.7% over the baseline and performs within 1.9% of the SOTA Fleming-VL. For text-based QA, it attains +6.9% over the baseline and +14.5% over Fleming-VL. In medical report generation, MedMO delivers significant gains in both semantic and clinical accuracy. Moreover, it exhibits strong grounding capability, achieving an IoU improvement of +40.4 over the baseline and +37.0% over Fleming-VL, underscoring its robust spatial reasoning and localization performance. Evaluations across radiology, ophthalmology, and pathology-microscopy confirm MedMO's broad cross-modality generalization. We release two versions of MedMO: 4B and 8B. Project is available at https://genmilab.github.io/MedMO-Page
description_zh: MedMO 旨在解决通用多模态大模型在医疗领域应用中的局限性，特别是领域覆盖不足、模态对齐偏差及定位推理能力弱的问题。该模型采用三阶段训练策略：首先进行跨模态预训练，将异构视觉编码器与医学语言骨干网络对齐；其次进行多任务指令微调，涵盖报告生成、视觉问答（VQA）及病灶定位；最后引入基于事实性检查与边界框GIoU奖励的强化学习，以增强临床场景下的空间定位与逻辑推理能力。实验表明，MedMO在多个医疗基准测试中表现优于现有开源模型，尤其在VQA和文本问答任务上实现了显著的准确率提升。

title: CineScene: Implicit 3D as Effective Scene Representation for Cinematic Video Generation
description_en: Cinematic video production requires control over scene-subject composition and camera movement, but live-action shooting remains costly due to the need for constructing physical sets. To address this, we introduce the task of cinematic video generation with decoupled scene context: given multiple images of a static environment, the goal is to synthesize high-quality videos featuring dynamic subject while preserving the underlying scene consistency and following a user-specified camera trajectory. We present CineScene, a framework that leverages implicit 3D-aware scene representation for cinematic video generation. Our key innovation is a novel context conditioning mechanism that injects 3D-aware features in an implicit way: By encoding scene images into visual representations through VGGT, CineScene injects spatial priors into a pretrained text-to-video generation model by additional context concatenation, enabling camera-controlled video synthesis with consistent scenes and dynamic subjects. To further enhance the model's robustness, we introduce a simple yet effective random-shuffling strategy for the input scene images during training. To address the lack of training data, we construct a scene-decoupled dataset with Unreal Engine 5, containing paired videos of scenes with and without dynamic subjects, panoramic images representing the underlying static scene, along with their camera trajectories. Experiments show that CineScene achieves state-of-the-art performance in scene-consistent cinematic video generation, handling large camera movements and demonstrating generalization across diverse environments.
description_zh: CineScene旨在解决电影制作中场景与主体解耦的难题。传统视频生成难以在保持静态环境一致性的同时实现复杂的动态主体运动和相机轨迹控制。该项目提出了一种隐式3D感知场景表示方法，通过VGGT编码器将多张静态场景图像转化为空间先验，并将其注入预训练的文生视频模型中。通过引入随机洗牌策略增强鲁棒性，该框架实现了在固定场景下对动态主体和相机运动的精准控制，降低了物理布景的成本。

title: Cochain Perspectives on Temporal-Difference Signals for Learning Beyond Markov Dynamics
description_en: Non-Markovian dynamics are commonly found in real-world environments due to long-range dependencies, partial observability, and memory effects. The Bellman equation that is the central pillar of Reinforcement learning (RL) becomes only approximately valid under Non-Markovian. Existing work often focus on practical algorithm designs and offer limited theoretical treatment to address key questions, such as what dynamics are indeed capturable by the Bellman framework and how to inspire new algorithm classes with optimal approximations. In this paper, we present a novel topological viewpoint on temporal-difference (TD) based RL. We show that TD errors can be viewed as 1-cochain in the topological space of state transitions, while Markov dynamics are then interpreted as topological integrability. This novel view enables us to obtain a Hodge-type decomposition of TD errors into an integrable component and a topological residual, through a Bellman-de Rham projection. We further propose HodgeFlow Policy Search (HFPS) by fitting a potential network to minimize the non-integrable projection residual in RL, achieving stability/sensitivity guarantees. In numerical evaluations, HFPS is shown to significantly improve RL performance under non-Markovian.
description_zh: 该研究针对强化学习（RL）中核心的贝尔曼方程在非马尔可夫环境下的失效问题，提出了一种全新的拓扑视角。研究者将时间差分（TD）误差解释为状态转换拓扑空间中的1-cochain，并将马尔可夫动力学视为拓扑可积性。通过引入Bellman-de Rham投影，实现了TD误差的Hodge型分解，将其拆解为可积成分与拓扑残差。在此基础上，作者提出了HodgeFlow Policy Search (HFPS) 算法，通过拟合势能网络最小化非可积残差，从而在处理长程依赖和部分可观测环境时提供更好的稳定性和灵敏度保证。

full_profile.one_liner: Cochain Perspectives on TD Signals — 一种基于拓扑学（Hodge分解）解决非马尔可夫强化学习难题的理论框架，由Zuyuan Zhang等提出。
full_profile.overview_zh: 该研究针对强化学习（RL）中核心的贝尔曼方程在非马尔可夫环境下的失效问题，提出了一种全新的拓扑视角。研究者将时间差分（TD）误差解释为状态转换拓扑空间中的1-cochain，并将马尔可夫动力学视为拓扑可积性。通过引入Bellman-de Rham投影，实现了TD误差的Hodge型分解，将其拆解为可积成分与拓扑残差。在此基础上，作者提出了HodgeFlow Policy Search (HFPS) 算法，通过拟合势能网络最小化非可积残差，从而在处理长程依赖和部分可观测环境时提供更好的稳定性和灵敏度保证。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 该项目为纯学术研究论文，目前无商业模式。其价值在于为强化学习算法的底层范式提供理论支撑，未来可能通过专利授权、算法库集成或作为复杂决策系统（如自动驾驶、机器人控制）的底层优化模块实现商业化，但目前尚处于理论验证阶段，无明确变现路径。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone视角：该方向的Alpha在于将代数拓扑引入RL，试图从数学本质上解决非马尔可夫动力学的建模难题，这属于RL领域的高阶理论突破。市场目前过度关注大模型Scaling Law，而忽略了决策智能在复杂动态环境下的数学基础瓶颈。风险在于该方法在复杂高维环境下的计算开销及泛化能力尚待验证，且从理论到工业级算法的转化周期极长。我们的投资论文倾向于关注能将此类数学突破转化为可工程化、可规模化部署的决策引擎的团队。

title: A Cycle-Consistent Graph Surrogate for Full-Cycle Left Ventricular Myocardial Biomechanics
description_en: Image-based patient-specific simulation of left ventricular (LV) mechanics is valuable for understanding cardiac function and supporting clinical intervention planning, but conventional finite-element analysis (FEA) is computationally intensive. Current graph-based surrogates do not have full-cycle prediction capabilities, and physics-informed neural networks often struggle to converge on complex cardiac geometries. We present CardioGraphFENet (CGFENet), a unified graph-based surrogate for rapid full-cycle estimation of LV myocardial biomechanics, supervised by a large FEA simulation dataset. The proposed model integrates (i) a global--local graph encoder to capture mesh features with weak-form-inspired global coupling, (ii) a gated recurrent unit-based temporal encoder conditioned on the target volume-time signal to model cycle-coherent dynamics, and (iii) a cycle-consistent bidirectional formulation for both loading and inverse unloading within a single framework. These strategies enable high fidelity with respect to traditional FEA ground truths and produce physiologically plausible pressure-volume loops that match FEA results when coupled with a lumped-parameter model. In particular, the cycle-consistency strategy enables a significant reduction in FEA supervision with only minimal loss in accuracy.
description_zh: CardioGraphFENet (CGFENet) 旨在解决传统有限元分析 (FEA) 在心脏生物力学模拟中计算成本过高的问题。该模型通过全局-局部图编码器捕捉复杂网格特征，结合GRU时间编码器处理心动周期动态，并引入循环一致性双向架构，实现了从加载到卸载的全周期模拟。其核心价值在于将复杂的物理模拟转化为高效的神经网络推理，在保持高保真度的同时，能够生成符合生理特征的压力-容积 (PV) 环，为临床干预规划提供实时辅助工具。

title: Agentic Uncertainty Reveals Agentic Overconfidence
description_en: Can AI agents predict whether they will succeed at a task? We study agentic uncertainty by eliciting success probability estimates before, during, and after task execution. All results exhibit agentic overconfidence: some agents that succeed only 22% of the time predict 77% success. Counterintuitively, pre-execution assessment with strictly less information tends to yield better discrimination than standard post-execution review, though differences are not always significant. Adversarial prompting reframing assessment as bug-finding achieves the best calibration.
description_zh: 该研究探讨了AI Agent在执行任务前、中、后对自身成功概率的预测能力。研究发现，Agent普遍存在严重的“代理过度自信”现象，例如某些实际成功率仅为22%的Agent，其自我预测成功率高达77%。研究通过对比不同阶段的评估发现，在执行前进行评估（信息较少时）反而比执行后的回顾具有更好的区分度。此外，研究证明通过对抗性提示词（将评估重构为“找Bug”过程）可以显著改善Agent的校准能力。该工作为提升Agent的可靠性和自我监控机制提供了重要的学术参考。

title: SEMA: Simple yet Effective Learning for Multi-Turn Jailbreak Attacks
description_en: Multi-turn jailbreaks capture the real threat model for safety-aligned chatbots, where single-turn attacks are merely a special case. Yet existing approaches break under exploration complexity and intent drift. We propose SEMA, a simple yet effective framework that trains a multi-turn attacker without relying on any existing strategies or external data. SEMA comprises two stages. Prefilling self-tuning enables usable rollouts by fine-tuning on non-refusal, well-structured, multi-turn adversarial prompts that are self-generated with a minimal prefix, thereby stabilizing subsequent learning. Reinforcement learning with intent-drift-aware reward trains the attacker to elicit valid multi-turn adversarial prompts while maintaining the same harmful objective. We anchor harmful intent in multi-turn jailbreaks via an intent-drift-aware reward that combines intent alignment, compliance risk, and level of detail. Our open-loop attack regime avoids dependence on victim feedback, unifies single- and multi-turn settings, and reduces exploration complexity. Across multiple datasets, victim models, and jailbreak judges, our method achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) attack success rates (ASR), outperforming all single-turn baselines, manually scripted and template-driven multi-turn baselines, as well as our SFT (Supervised Fine-Tuning) and DPO (Direct Preference Optimization) variants. For instance, SEMA performs an average $80.1\%$ ASR@1 across three closed-source and open-source victim models on AdvBench, 33.9% over SOTA. The approach is compact, reproducible, and transfers across targets, providing a stronger and more realistic stress test for large language model (LLM) safety and enabling automatic redteaming to expose and localize failure modes. Our code is available at: https://github.com/fmmarkmq/SEMA.
description_zh: SEMA (Simple yet Effective Learning for Multi-Turn Jailbreak Attacks) 旨在解决大模型安全对齐中的多轮越狱难题。现有攻击方法常受限于探索复杂度和多轮对话中的意图漂移。SEMA采用两阶段训练：首先通过预填充自调优（Prefilling self-tuning）生成结构化、非拒绝的多轮对抗提示，稳定学习过程；其次利用意图漂移感知奖励（Intent-drift-aware reward）进行强化学习，在保持有害目标一致性的前提下，通过对齐意图、合规风险和细节程度来优化攻击策略。该方法无需依赖受害者模型的反馈，实现了单轮与多轮攻击的统一，显著提升了攻击成功率（ASR）。

full_profile.overview_zh: SEMA (Simple yet Effective Learning for Multi-Turn Jailbreak Attacks) 旨在解决大模型安全对齐中的多轮越狱难题。现有攻击方法常受限于探索复杂度和多轮对话中的意图漂移。SEMA采用两阶段训练：首先通过预填充自调优（Prefilling self-tuning）生成结构化、非拒绝的多轮对抗提示，稳定学习过程；其次利用意图漂移感知奖励（Intent-drift-aware reward）进行强化学习，在保持有害目标一致性的前提下，通过对齐意图、合规风险和细节程度来优化攻击策略。该方法无需依赖受害者模型的反馈，实现了单轮与多轮攻击的统一，显著提升了攻击成功率（ASR）。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 该项目为纯学术研究成果，目前无商业化计划。作为开源论文，其价值在于为红队测试（Red Teaming）和模型安全防御提供基准工具。若转化为商业产品，可作为企业级大模型安全评估平台的底层算法引擎，通过API授权或安全审计服务变现。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone视角：AI安全领域（AI Safety/Red Teaming）是模型部署的刚需，但纯攻击算法研究往往难以直接转化为商业闭环。SEMA的Alpha在于其对多轮对话复杂性的解构，特别是意图漂移的量化处理。然而，该方向面临极大的政策与伦理风险，且防御技术（如防御性微调）的迭代速度往往快于攻击算法。我们认为此类技术更适合作为防御性研究的基石，而非独立的商业投资标的，除非能将其转化为自动化的防御加固工具。

title: The Representational Geometry of Number
description_en: A central question in cognitive science is whether conceptual representations converge onto a shared manifold to support generalization, or diverge into orthogonal subspaces to minimize task interference. While prior work has discovered evidence for both, a mechanistic account of how these properties coexist and transform across tasks remains elusive. We propose that representational sharing lies not in the concepts themselves, but in the geometric relations between them. Using number concepts as a testbed and language models as high-dimensional computational substrates, we show that number representations preserve a stable relational structure across tasks. Task-specific representations are embedded in distinct subspaces, with low-level features like magnitude and parity encoded along separable linear directions. Crucially, we find that these subspaces are largely transformable into one another via linear mappings, indicating that representations share relational structure despite being located in distinct subspaces. Together, these results provide a mechanistic lens of how language models balance the shared structure of number representation with functional flexibility. It suggests that understanding arises when task-specific transformations are applied to a shared underlying relational structure of conceptual representations.
description_zh: 该研究探讨了认知科学中的核心问题：概念表征是在共享流形上收敛，还是在正交子空间中发散以减少任务干扰。研究以数字概念为测试基准，利用大语言模型（LLM）作为高维计算底座，发现数字表征在不同任务中保持了稳定的关系结构。核心发现包括：任务特定的表征嵌入在不同的子空间中，但这些子空间可以通过线性映射相互转换。这意味着LLM通过保持几何关系的一致性，实现了功能灵活性与结构共享的平衡。该成果为理解LLM内部表征的机制提供了新的几何视角，有助于优化模型在多任务学习中的泛化能力。

title: Reliable Mislabel Detection for Video Capsule Endoscopy Data
description_en: The classification performance of deep neural networks relies strongly on access to large, accurately annotated datasets. In medical imaging, however, obtaining such datasets is particularly challenging since annotations must be provided by specialized physicians, which severely limits the pool of annotators. Furthermore, class boundaries can often be ambiguous or difficult to define which further complicates machine learning-based classification. In this paper, we want to address this problem and introduce a framework for mislabel detection in medical datasets. This is validated on the two largest, publicly available datasets for Video Capsule Endoscopy, an important imaging procedure for examining the gastrointestinal tract based on a video stream of lowresolution images. In addition, potentially mislabeled samples identified by our pipeline were reviewed and re-annotated by three experienced gastroenterologists. Our results show that the proposed framework successfully detects incorrectly labeled data and results in an improved anomaly detection performance after cleaning the datasets compared to current baselines.
description_zh: 该项目针对医疗影像（特别是胶囊内镜Video Capsule Endoscopy）中高质量标注数据稀缺且昂贵的问题，提出了一种自动化的错误标注检测框架。其核心机制在于通过算法识别训练集中的潜在错误标签，并利用三位资深胃肠科医生进行复核与重标注。该方法通过清洗数据集，显著提升了后续异常检测模型的性能。该研究不仅解决了医疗AI中常见的“脏数据”问题，还为医学影像数据集的质量控制提供了一套可量化的技术路径。

title: From Core to Detail: Unsupervised Disentanglement with Entropy-Ordered Flows
description_en: Learning unsupervised representations that are both semantically meaningful and stable across runs remains a central challenge in modern representation learning. We introduce entropy-ordered flows (EOFlows), a normalizing-flow framework that orders latent dimensions by their explained entropy, analogously to PCA's explained variance. This ordering enables adaptive injective flows: after training, one may retain only the top C latent variables to form a compact core representation while the remaining variables capture fine-grained detail and noise, with C chosen flexibly at inference time rather than fixed during training. EOFlows build on insights from Independent Mechanism Analysis, Principal Component Flows and Manifold Entropic Metrics. We combine likelihood-based training with local Jacobian regularization and noise augmentation into a method that scales well to high-dimensional data such as images. Experiments on the CelebA dataset show that our method uncovers a rich set of semantically interpretable features, allowing for high compression and strong denoising.
description_zh: 该项目提出了一种名为“熵排序流”（Entropy-Ordered Flows, EOFlows）的算法框架，旨在解决无监督表征学习中语义可解释性与稳定性不足的问题。其核心机制类似于PCA的方差解释，通过对潜在维度进行熵排序，实现自适应注入流。该方法允许在推理阶段灵活选择保留前C个潜在变量，从而在保留核心语义表征的同时，将噪声与细节分离。该技术结合了独立机制分析（IMA）与局部雅可比正则化，在CelebA等高维图像数据集上展示了良好的语义解耦与压缩能力，为生成模型的可解释性提供了新的数学范式。

title: Revisiting the Generic Transformer: Deconstructing a Strong Baseline for Time Series Foundation Models
description_en: The recent surge in Time Series Foundation Models has rapidly advanced the field, yet the heterogeneous training setups across studies make it difficult to attribute improvements to architectural innovations versus data engineering. In this work, we investigate the potential of a standard patch Transformer, demonstrating that this generic architecture achieves state-of-the-art zero-shot forecasting performance using a straightforward training protocol. We conduct a comprehensive ablation study that covers model scaling, data composition, and training techniques to isolate the essential ingredients for high performance. Our findings identify the key drivers of performance, while confirming that the generic architecture itself demonstrates excellent scalability. By strictly controlling these variables, we provide comprehensive empirical results on model scaling across multiple dimensions. We release our open-source model and detailed findings to establish a transparent, reproducible baseline for future research.
description_zh: 该研究针对当前时间序列基础模型（TSFM）领域中训练设置高度异构、难以区分架构创新与数据工程贡献的问题，进行了系统性解构。研究团队通过严格控制变量，证明了一个标准的Patch Transformer架构在经过优化的训练协议下，即可达到当前时间序列零样本预测的SOTA水平。论文通过全面的消融实验，详细分析了模型缩放、数据组成及训练技术对性能的影响，旨在为后续研究提供一个透明、可复现的基准，而非盲目追求复杂的架构堆叠。

full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone视角：该研究揭示了当前AI领域的一个重要Alpha信号——即在模型架构趋于同质化的背景下，‘数据工程与训练协议’的权重远高于‘架构创新’。市场目前过度追捧复杂的架构设计，而低估了标准化基准的价值。风险在于该研究本质上是学术性的，缺乏产品化闭环，且时间序列领域竞争激烈，通用架构的护城河较浅。我们的投资论文倾向于寻找能够将此类基准转化为垂直行业（如能源、金融）预测Agent的团队，而非单纯的架构研究。

title: A first realization of reinforcement learning-based closed-loop EEG-TMS
description_en: Background: Transcranial magnetic stimulation (TMS) is a powerful tool to investigate neurophysiology of the human brain and treat brain disorders. Traditionally, therapeutic TMS has been applied in a one-size-fits-all approach, disregarding inter- and intra-individual differences. Brain state-dependent EEG-TMS, such as coupling TMS with a pre-specified phase of the sensorimotor mu-rhythm, enables the induction of differential neuroplastic effects depending on the targeted phase. But this approach is still user-dependent as it requires defining an a-priori target phase. Objectives: To present a first realization of a machine-learning-based, closed-loop real-time EEG-TMS setup to identify user-independently the individual mu-rhythm phase associated with high- vs. low-corticospinal excitability states. Methods: We applied EEG-TMS to 25 participants targeting the supplementary motor area-primary motor cortex network and used a reinforcement learning algorithm to identify the mu-rhythm phase associated with high- vs. low corticospinal excitability. We employed linear mixed effects models and Bayesian analysis to determine effects of reinforced learning on corticospinal excitability indexed by motor evoked potential amplitude, and functional connectivity indexed by the imaginary part of resting-state EEG coherence. Results: Reinforcement learning effectively identified the mu-rhythm phase associated with high- vs. low-excitability states, and their repetitive stimulation resulted in long-term increases vs. decreases in functional connectivity in the stimulated sensorimotor network. Conclusions: We demonstrated for the first time the feasibility of closed-loop EEG-TMS in humans, a critical step towards individualized treatment of brain disorders.

title: Sample Complexity of Causal Identification with Temporal Heterogeneity
description_en: Recovering a unique causal graph from observational data is an ill-posed problem because multiple generating mechanisms can lead to the same observational distribution. This problem becomes solvable only by exploiting specific structural or distributional assumptions. While recent work has separately utilized time-series dynamics or multi-environment heterogeneity to constrain this problem, we integrate both as complementary sources of heterogeneity. This integration yields unified necessary identifiability conditions and enables a rigorous analysis of the statistical limits of recovery under thin versus heavy-tailed noise. In particular, temporal structure is shown to effectively substitute for missing environmental diversity, possibly achieving identifiability even under insufficient heterogeneity. Extending this analysis to heavy-tailed (Student's t) distributions, we demonstrate that while geometric identifiability conditions remain invariant, the sample complexity diverges significantly from the Gaussian baseline. Explicit information-theoretic bounds quantify this cost of robustness, establishing the fundamental limits of covariance-based causal graph recovery methods in realistic non-stationary systems. This work shifts the focus from whether causal structure is identifiable to whether it is statistically recoverable in practice.
description_zh: 该论文针对因果图发现（Causal Discovery）中的核心难题——观测数据下的因果不可识别性，提出了一种理论框架。研究通过将时间序列动态与多环境异质性相结合，推导出了统一的识别条件。核心贡献在于：1. 证明了时间结构可以弥补环境多样性的不足；2. 建立了在薄尾（高斯）与重尾（Student's t）噪声分布下，因果图恢复的样本复杂度理论界限。该研究为因果推断算法的鲁棒性设计提供了数学基石，特别是在处理复杂、非平稳真实世界数据时的统计极限分析。

title: Vision Transformer Finetuning Benefits from Non-Smooth Components
description_en: The smoothness of the transformer architecture has been extensively studied in the context of generalization, training stability, and adversarial robustness. However, its role in transfer learning remains poorly understood. In this paper, we analyze the ability of vision transformer components to adapt their outputs to changes in inputs, or, in other words, their plasticity. Defined as an average rate of change, it captures the sensitivity to input perturbation; in particular, a high plasticity implies low smoothness. We demonstrate through theoretical analysis and comprehensive experiments that this perspective provides principled guidance in choosing the components to prioritize during adaptation. A key takeaway for practitioners is that the high plasticity of the attention modules and feedforward layers consistently leads to better finetuning performance. Our findings depart from the prevailing assumption that smoothness is desirable, offering a novel perspective on the functional properties of transformers. The code is available at https://github.com/ambroiseodt/vit-plasticity.
description_zh: 该研究挑战了深度学习领域中“平滑度（Smoothness）即优良属性”的传统共识。研究团队通过定义“塑性（Plasticity）”这一指标，量化了Vision Transformer（ViT）组件对输入扰动的敏感度。研究发现，在微调过程中，注意力模块（Attention Modules）和前馈网络（FFN）表现出的高塑性（即低平滑度）反而能显著提升迁移学习的性能。该论文通过理论推导与大规模实验，为ViT架构的参数高效微调（PEFT）提供了新的指导原则，即在微调时应优先选择具有高塑性的组件进行更新，而非盲目追求平滑。

title: T-STAR: A Context-Aware Transformer Framework for Short-Term Probabilistic Demand Forecasting in Dock-Based Shared Micro-Mobility
description_en: Reliable short-term demand forecasting is essential for managing shared micro-mobility services and ensuring responsive, user-centered operations. This study introduces T-STAR (Two-stage Spatial and Temporal Adaptive contextual Representation), a novel transformer-based probabilistic framework designed to forecast station-level bike-sharing demand at a 15-minute resolution. T-STAR addresses key challenges in high-resolution forecasting by disentangling consistent demand patterns from short-term fluctuations through a hierarchical two-stage structure. The first stage captures coarse-grained hourly demand patterns, while the second stage improves prediction accuracy by incorporating high-frequency, localized inputs, including recent fluctuations and real-time demand variations in connected metro services, to account for temporal shifts in short-term demand. Time series transformer models are employed in both stages to generate probabilistic predictions. Extensive experiments using Washington D.C.'s Capital Bikeshare data demonstrate that T-STAR outperforms existing methods in both deterministic and probabilistic accuracy. The model exhibits strong spatial and temporal robustness across stations and time periods. A zero-shot forecasting experiment further highlights T-STAR's ability to transfer to previously unseen service areas without retraining. These results underscore the framework's potential to deliver granular, reliable, and uncertainty-aware short-term demand forecasts, which enable seamless integration to support multimodal trip planning for travelers and enhance real-time operations in shared micro-mobility services.
description_zh: T-STAR (Two-stage Spatial and Temporal Adaptive contextual Representation) 旨在解决共享微出行（如共享单车）在站点层面的短期需求预测难题。该模型采用两阶段架构：第一阶段通过Transformer捕捉小时级的宏观需求模式；第二阶段引入高频局部输入（如近期波动及地铁实时数据），通过时空自适应机制对短期需求进行精细化建模。该框架不仅提供确定性预测，还通过概率预测量化不确定性，在华盛顿特区Capital Bikeshare数据集上验证了其优于现有基准模型的性能。

full_profile.overview_zh: T-STAR (Two-stage Spatial and Temporal Adaptive contextual Representation) 旨在解决共享微出行（如共享单车）在站点层面的短期需求预测难题。该模型采用两阶段架构：第一阶段通过Transformer捕捉小时级的宏观需求模式；第二阶段引入高频局部输入（如近期波动及地铁实时数据），通过时空自适应机制对短期需求进行精细化建模。该框架不仅提供确定性预测，还通过概率预测量化不确定性，在华盛顿特区Capital Bikeshare数据集上验证了其优于现有基准模型的性能。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 该项目目前为纯学术研究，暂无商业化计划。若转化为商业产品，其逻辑应为向共享出行运营商（如Lime, Bird, 美团单车）提供SaaS化的调度优化API，通过提升车辆调度效率、降低运营成本（如减少空车率和缺车率）来获取订阅费或按调用量付费。
full_profile.insight_zh: 在Track Tech视角下，T-STAR属于典型的垂直领域算法优化。其Alpha在于将Transformer架构应用于高频时空序列预测，通过解耦长短期模式提升了鲁棒性。然而，该方向面临严重的同质化竞争，且此类预测模型往往难以脱离特定城市的数据分布，泛化能力是其核心风险。对于Creekstone而言，除非该算法能被封装为通用的时空预测引擎并解决冷启动问题，否则其作为独立商业实体的投资价值有限。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native得12分，因为其核心是算法模型而非Agent工作流；Niche得14分，共享出行调度是刚需但市场相对封闭；商业模式得5分，目前仅为学术论文，无变现路径；团队得9分，学术背景合理但缺乏产业化经验；扣分项主要在于信息严重不足（-3）以及纯学术项目缺乏商业落地验证（-5），导致整体评分处于较低水平。
full_profile.metrics_summary: 暂无公开数据

title: One Minute News
description_en: In today’s internet, headlines have become traps. They tease. They mislead. They make you click, only to find that the story is nothing like what you expected. We built oneminutenews.org as a response to the clickbait age. Our mission is simple: Give you the news in straight forward language and rank them based on their importance. The result? Clear, honest, no-fluff headlines that tell you exactly what happened, the way real journalism used to.
description_zh: One Minute News 旨在对抗现代互联网中泛滥的“标题党”现象。该产品通过AI技术对海量新闻进行筛选、重写与排序，将复杂且具有误导性的新闻标题转化为直观、无废话的简短摘要。其核心机制在于利用算法评估新闻的真实重要性，而非点击率，从而为用户提供高效的信息获取体验。目前该产品已在Product Hunt上线并获得一定关注，但其底层AI模型是否具备深度Agent能力或个性化推荐闭环尚不明确，主要表现为一种基于LLM的工具型应用。
track_reason: 面向C端用户，提供新闻阅读服务。

plus: 解决了用户对信息过载的痛点
产品界面简洁，符合极简主义趋势
minus: 产品同质化严重，极易被大模型原生功能取代

full_profile.one_liner: One Minute News — AI驱动的新闻摘要与去标题党平台，旨在通过简洁语言还原新闻真相，创始人背景信息缺失。
full_profile.overview_zh: One Minute News 旨在对抗现代互联网中泛滥的“标题党”现象。该产品通过AI技术对海量新闻进行筛选、重写与排序，将复杂且具有误导性的新闻标题转化为直观、无废话的简短摘要。其核心机制在于利用算法评估新闻的真实重要性，而非点击率，从而为用户提供高效的信息获取体验。目前该产品已在Product Hunt上线并获得一定关注，但其底层AI模型是否具备深度Agent能力或个性化推荐闭环尚不明确，主要表现为一种基于LLM的工具型应用。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 目前商业模式尚不清晰，主要处于免费获取用户阶段。潜在的商业路径包括订阅制（Premium News Feed）或通过提供高质量、无广告的阅读环境吸引付费用户。由于新闻聚合类产品面临极高的同质化竞争，且缺乏独家数据源，其定价逻辑目前难以支撑高溢价，与传统新闻App相比，其商业化路径缺乏显著的差异化优势。
full_profile.insight_zh: 在信息过载时代，用户对“去噪”服务的需求确实存在，但该赛道已是红海。One Minute News 的 Alpha 信号较弱，因为其核心逻辑（摘要+去标题党）极易被主流新闻聚合器（如Apple News, Google News）或通用大模型（ChatGPT/Perplexity）通过插件功能覆盖。该项目的风险在于缺乏护城河，且新闻版权与合规性风险较高。Creekstone认为，若无独特的私有数据飞轮或极强的用户留存数据，该项目难以形成长期的投资价值。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native得16分，因为其核心功能依赖LLM进行摘要，但缺乏Agent工作流；Niche得11分，因为去标题党是伪需求，极易被大模型原生功能替代；商业模式得8分，因为目前缺乏明确的变现计划；团队得8分，因为缺乏公开的创始人背景信息，存在信息不对称风险；扣分项-5分，主要因为产品同质化严重且缺乏核心技术壁垒，属于典型的Prompt工程应用，难以在激烈的Lifestyle赛道中脱颖而出。
full_profile.metrics_summary: Product Hunt: 157票，暂无其他公开数据（如ARR、DAU、GitHub stars）。

title: Sunday
description_en: How many great ideas have you highlighted, only to close the book and never see them again? We all do it. We dog-ear pages, snap messy photos that get lost in our camera roll, or scribble in margins. We tell ourselves we’ll remember that profound sentence, but we rarely do. Sunday fixes this. It is a purpose-built tool designed to make capturing wisdom as seamless as reading it. Reading is an investment of your time and attention. Sunday ensures you get to keep the returns.
description_zh: Sunday 旨在解决用户阅读书籍时“读过即忘”的痛点。用户通过拍照或手动输入，将书中的金句、观点摘录至应用内。产品核心功能包括：OCR文字识别、笔记分类与标签管理、以及定期回顾提醒。它试图将碎片化的阅读体验转化为可检索的个人知识库。目前产品处于早期阶段，主要通过解决“相机胶卷中杂乱照片”和“书页折角”的低效问题来提供价值，但在AI深度集成（如自动总结、关联推荐、知识图谱构建）方面尚显不足。
track_reason: 面向C端，帮助用户记录和管理想法，属于个人应用。

score_breakdown.reason: 产品功能过于基础，缺乏AI Native壁垒，且面临强力竞品挤压，属于典型的工具类应用，缺乏Alpha潜力。
plus: 解决了阅读笔记碎片化存储的真实痛点
产品界面设计简洁，符合Lifestyle定位
minus: 缺乏AI深度集成，仅为传统工具的数字化升级
面临Readwise等成熟竞品的直接竞争
创始人背景及团队信息严重缺失
商业模式不清晰，缺乏高价值用户留存机制

full_profile.one_liner: Sunday — 一款专注于书籍金句摘录与管理的工具，通过OCR与结构化存储解决阅读笔记遗忘问题，创始人背景信息有限。
full_profile.overview_zh: Sunday 旨在解决用户阅读书籍时“读过即忘”的痛点。用户通过拍照或手动输入，将书中的金句、观点摘录至应用内。产品核心功能包括：OCR文字识别、笔记分类与标签管理、以及定期回顾提醒。它试图将碎片化的阅读体验转化为可检索的个人知识库。目前产品处于早期阶段，主要通过解决“相机胶卷中杂乱照片”和“书页折角”的低效问题来提供价值，但在AI深度集成（如自动总结、关联推荐、知识图谱构建）方面尚显不足。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 目前商业模式尚不清晰，推测为Freemium模式，即基础摘录功能免费，高级功能（如无限存储、多设备同步、导出格式、AI智能分类）通过订阅制收费。定价逻辑基于用户对知识留存的刚需，但由于此类工具极易被Notion、Obsidian或Readwise等成熟生态替代，其付费转化率面临较大挑战。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone视角：阅读笔记管理是一个典型的“窄门”市场，虽然用户痛点真实，但该领域已存在Readwise等强力竞品，且Notion等通用工具通过AI插件已覆盖大部分需求。Sunday目前的Alpha信号较弱，缺乏独特的AI Native交互（如基于上下文的深度洞察或主动式知识关联）。我们认为该方向若要突围，必须从单纯的“摘录工具”进化为“知识Agent”，否则极易沦为工具箱中的边缘应用。风险在于用户留存率低，且缺乏护城河。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native得16分，仅具备基础OCR功能，未见Agent工作流；Niche得12分，场景明确但同质化严重；商业模式得8分，变现路径模糊；团队得8分，缺乏公开背景信息；减分项主要针对信息严重不足（-3）及产品缺乏核心壁垒（-3）。总分48分，属于兴趣一般，难以看到显著的Alpha增长潜力，建议保持关注但无需投入资源。
full_profile.metrics_summary: Product Hunt: 121票，暂无其他公开数据。

title: Helpmaton
description_en: Helpmaton is the workspace-based platform to build, manage, and scale AI agents without the chaos. Organize agents into dedicated workspaces with shared knowledge, custom budgets, and team permissions. 🚀 Key Features: • Memory & Docs: Agents learn from chats and your files. • Native Integrations: Google Workspace & Notion (OAuth). • Deployment: Slack/Discord bots. • Multi-Agent: Task delegation & web search. • Flexibility: BYO API keys or use ours. Open-source ready.
description_zh: Helpmaton 是一个旨在为企业提供结构化 Agent 管理的平台。其核心逻辑是将 Agent 部署在独立的工作空间（Workspace）中，通过统一的知识库管理（支持文件上传与聊天记录学习）、权限控制以及预算管理，试图解决企业内部 Agent 碎片化的问题。产品支持 Google Workspace 和 Notion 的 OAuth 集成，并能将 Agent 快速部署至 Slack 或 Discord。此外，它还支持多 Agent 协作与任务委派，允许用户自带 API Key 或使用平台提供的服务，定位为企业级 Agent 基础设施。
track_reason: 构建、管理和扩展AI Agent的平台，面向开发者。

score_breakdown.reason: 产品处于极度同质化的 Agent 平台赛道，缺乏社区数据支撑与技术壁垒，属于典型的平庸型项目。
plus: 支持主流办公套件集成
具备基础的多 Agent 协作功能
minus: Product Hunt 票数极低，社区认可度不足
缺乏核心技术壁垒

full_profile.one_liner: Helpmaton — 一个面向团队的AI Agent工作空间管理平台，提供知识库共享与多Agent协作，旨在解决企业内部Agent部署的混乱问题。
full_profile.overview_zh: Helpmaton 是一个旨在为企业提供结构化 Agent 管理的平台。其核心逻辑是将 Agent 部署在独立的工作空间（Workspace）中，通过统一的知识库管理（支持文件上传与聊天记录学习）、权限控制以及预算管理，试图解决企业内部 Agent 碎片化的问题。产品支持 Google Workspace 和 Notion 的 OAuth 集成，并能将 Agent 快速部署至 Slack 或 Discord。此外，它还支持多 Agent 协作与任务委派，允许用户自带 API Key 或使用平台提供的服务，定位为企业级 Agent 基础设施。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 目前商业模式尚不清晰，主要采取 SaaS 订阅制或 API 使用费模式。通过提供企业级权限管理和知识库同步来吸引付费用户。然而，由于缺乏明确的差异化定价策略，且在开源 Agent 框架（如 LangChain/Dify）的挤压下，其商业逻辑的护城河较弱，难以证明其在企业级市场的高溢价能力。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone 视角：Agent 管理平台（Agent Ops）是当前的热门赛道，但 Helpmaton 处于极度拥挤的红海中。其核心风险在于产品缺乏深度的技术壁垒，功能高度同质化，且缺乏像 Dify 那样强大的开源社区驱动力。市场目前更倾向于选择能够深度集成现有工作流的成熟工具，而非一个新兴的中间件。该项目的 Alpha 信号极弱，缺乏真实付费用户数据支撑，且在开发者生态中未见显著影响力，投资性价比极低。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native 得 16 分，具备基础的 Agent 编排与知识库集成，但缺乏自进化闭环；Niche 得 12 分，定位企业级管理，但同类竞品过多，缺乏护城河；商业模式得 8 分，仅有基础订阅逻辑，缺乏高价值用户验证；团队得 7 分，缺乏公开背景信息，无法评估其技术实力；加减分项中，因 Product Hunt 极低的人气（16 票）及缺乏关键增长指标，给予 -5 分惩罚。整体处于执行层面的平庸产品，缺乏投资价值。

title: Taskmelt - AI Task Planner
description_en: Your brain moves faster than you can type. TaskMelt lets you speak your tasks and the AI instantly organizes everything into clean, actionable lists. No typing. No categorizing. No setup. Just talk. Perfect for ADHD minds, busy parents, and anyone drowning in mental to-do lists. → Voice-first capture (under 3 seconds) → AI auto-categorization → Zero friction design Built by a developer with ADHD who got tired of apps that don't work for real brains.
description_zh: Taskmelt 是一款主打“语音优先”的个人任务管理工具。其核心逻辑在于消除传统任务管理应用中繁琐的输入和分类步骤，用户只需通过语音描述杂乱的任务，AI 即可自动进行语义解析、分类并生成可执行的清单。该产品主要面向 ADHD 患者、忙碌的家长以及对传统 To-Do 应用感到疲惫的群体。目前产品处于早期阶段，主要通过降低认知负荷来提升用户留存，但尚未展示出复杂的工作流自动化或跨平台集成能力，产品形态更接近于一个轻量级的语音转清单工具。

full_profile.one_liner: Taskmelt — 语音驱动的AI任务规划器，通过语音转结构化清单解决ADHD人群的任务管理痛点，由ADHD开发者独立开发。
full_profile.overview_zh: Taskmelt 是一款主打“语音优先”的个人任务管理工具。其核心逻辑在于消除传统任务管理应用中繁琐的输入和分类步骤，用户只需通过语音描述杂乱的任务，AI 即可自动进行语义解析、分类并生成可执行的清单。该产品主要面向 ADHD 患者、忙碌的家长以及对传统 To-Do 应用感到疲惫的群体。目前产品处于早期阶段，主要通过降低认知负荷来提升用户留存，但尚未展示出复杂的工作流自动化或跨平台集成能力，产品形态更接近于一个轻量级的语音转清单工具。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 目前商业模式尚不明确，推测为典型的订阅制（SaaS）或 Freemium 模式。定价逻辑基于用户对“减少认知摩擦”的付费意愿。然而，该领域竞品极多（如 Todoist, Notion, TickTick 等），Taskmelt 若无更深度的 AI 自动化工作流或生态整合，很难在存量市场中建立高溢价的商业壁垒。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone 视角：个人任务管理是一个极度拥挤的红海市场。Taskmelt 的切入点（ADHD/语音优先）虽然具有一定的人文关怀和场景针对性，但技术壁垒极低，本质上是调用 LLM API 进行简单的文本结构化处理。市场低估了该类产品的用户流失率，因为用户往往在新鲜感过后会回归更成熟的生态系统。我们的投资论文倾向于寻找具备“Proactive Agent”能力、能主动规划并执行任务的工具，而 Taskmelt 目前仅停留在“Capture”阶段，缺乏足够的 Alpha 信号。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native 得 16 分，因为其核心流程仅是简单的语音转结构化，缺乏 Agent 闭环；Niche 得 12 分，虽然切中 ADHD 痛点，但该领域竞品极多，护城河薄弱；商业模式得 8 分，目前无明确变现路径，且面临巨头降维打击；团队得 9 分，背景合理但缺乏规模化运营经验；扣分项主要在于 Product Hunt 极低的人气（10 票）反映出市场验证不足，且产品形态过于单一，存在严重的同质化风险，难以支撑高估值。

title: Orcha
description_en: Orcha lets you build and orchestrate teams of specialized AI agents for software development. Visual workflow builder, pre-built agent templates, and a unified dashboard - all running 100% locally with your own API keys. Your code, your data, your control.
description_zh: Orcha 是一个专注于软件开发场景的 AI Agent 编排工具。其核心价值在于提供一个可视化工作流构建器，允许开发者通过拖拽方式编排多个专用 Agent 协同工作。产品主打“100%本地运行”和“数据隐私”，用户通过配置自己的 API Key 来驱动 Agent，避免了代码和数据上传至第三方云端。它内置了针对软件开发任务的预设模板，旨在降低复杂 Agent 系统构建的门槛，让开发者能够在一个统一的仪表盘中监控 Agent 的执行状态。目前该产品处于早期阶段，市场声量极小，缺乏大规模用户验证。

full_profile.one_liner: Orcha — 面向软件开发的本地化AI Agent编排平台，提供可视化工作流构建与模板，强调数据隐私与本地运行，创始人背景信息缺失。
full_profile.overview_zh: Orcha 是一个专注于软件开发场景的 AI Agent 编排工具。其核心价值在于提供一个可视化工作流构建器，允许开发者通过拖拽方式编排多个专用 Agent 协同工作。产品主打“100%本地运行”和“数据隐私”，用户通过配置自己的 API Key 来驱动 Agent，避免了代码和数据上传至第三方云端。它内置了针对软件开发任务的预设模板，旨在降低复杂 Agent 系统构建的门槛，让开发者能够在一个统一的仪表盘中监控 Agent 的执行状态。目前该产品处于早期阶段，市场声量极小，缺乏大规模用户验证。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 目前商业模式尚不清晰，推测为典型的开发者工具订阅制或开源免费+企业版付费模式。由于其强调本地运行和自带 API Key，其商业逻辑可能依赖于提供更高级的编排功能或企业级协作管理。相比于 Dify 或 LangChain 等成熟平台，Orcha 在商业化路径上缺乏差异化优势，且难以通过 API 调用抽成获利。
full_profile.insight_zh: 在 Agent 编排领域，Orcha 试图切入“隐私优先”的开发者工具赛道。虽然本地化运行是应对企业数据安全担忧的有效手段，但该领域竞争极其激烈（如 OpenHands, Cursor, Dify 等）。Orcha 的 Alpha 信号极弱，缺乏独特的底层技术壁垒或社区护城河。市场目前更倾向于能够提供深度 IDE 集成或强大生态支持的工具，Orcha 仅提供可视化编排，在功能深度和开发者留存上存在巨大挑战。投资风险在于其可能沦为功能单一的“玩具级”工具，难以在生产环境中替代现有的成熟框架。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native 得 16 分，具备基本的 Agent 编排能力，但缺乏自进化和深度工作流重构；Niche 得 12 分，定位在本地化开发 Agent，但同质化严重；商业模式得 8 分，目前无明确变现路径；团队得 8 分，因缺乏公开背景信息，按最低标准给分；减分项包括 PH 票数极低（<100票）且信息严重不足，扣除 6 分。综合来看，该项目处于早期探索阶段，缺乏足够的市场验证和技术壁垒，不建议作为重点关注对象。

full_profile.overview_zh: RexIDE 定位为开发者的 AI Agent 指挥中心，旨在解决多模型、多项目环境下 AI 工具碎片化的问题。其核心功能包括：集成 OpenCode、Claude、Codex 等主流模型，提供统一的终端管理、代码仓库监控、自动化的代码差异分析（diff insights）以及语音输入功能。该产品试图通过一个统一的界面将 AI Agent 的上下文保持在“热启动”状态，从而提升开发者的交付效率。目前该产品在 Product Hunt 上仅获得 9 票，处于极早期验证阶段，缺乏大规模用户验证或技术壁垒的明确证据。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 目前尚未披露明确的商业化路径。推测可能采取 SaaS 订阅模式，通过提供高级 AI 编排功能或集成付费 API 接口来获取收入。由于缺乏差异化的商业逻辑，其定价策略尚不清晰，且在面对 Cursor 等深度集成 IDE 的强力竞争下，其付费意愿转化存在较大挑战。

title: Indie Panel
description_en: Connect your Neon, Supabase, or PostgreSQL databases and track user metrics across all your indie projects from one dashboard. Real-time stats, daily snapshots, growth charts, and AES-256 encrypted connections.
description_zh: Indie Panel 是一款面向独立开发者（Indie Hackers）的轻量级数据库监控工具。它通过集成 Neon、Supabase 或标准 PostgreSQL 数据库，将分散在不同项目中的用户指标、增长数据和数据库快照聚合到一个统一的仪表盘中。产品核心功能包括实时统计、每日数据快照、增长趋势图表以及基于 AES-256 的加密连接。该产品旨在解决独立开发者在维护多个小型项目时，难以快速获取跨项目核心业务指标的痛点，通过可视化手段简化数据运营流程。

full_profile.overview_zh: Indie Panel 是一款面向独立开发者（Indie Hackers）的轻量级数据库监控工具。它通过集成 Neon、Supabase 或标准 PostgreSQL 数据库，将分散在不同项目中的用户指标、增长数据和数据库快照聚合到一个统一的仪表盘中。产品核心功能包括实时统计、每日数据快照、增长趋势图表以及基于 AES-256 的加密连接。该产品旨在解决独立开发者在维护多个小型项目时，难以快速获取跨项目核心业务指标的痛点，通过可视化手段简化数据运营流程。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 目前商业模式尚不清晰，推测为订阅制（SaaS）或免费增值模式。其定价逻辑基于开发者管理的项目数量或数据存储量。用户愿意付费的原因在于节省了手动构建监控看板的时间，并能更直观地看到业务增长。与竞品相比，其商业模式缺乏差异化，目前处于早期探索阶段，尚未形成规模化营收。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone视角：虽然开发者工具（DevTools）领域存在长尾需求，但Indie Panel所处的监控仪表盘赛道竞争极其激烈，且面临来自Datadog、Grafana等巨头以及Supabase原生监控功能的挤压。该方向的Alpha在于能否通过极简的配置和针对独立开发者的特定指标（如MRR关联）建立壁垒，但目前产品形态过于通用，缺乏AI驱动的洞察或自动化运维能力。风险在于产品功能过于单一，极易被云厂商原生功能覆盖，投资论文难以支撑其作为独立高增长标的。

full_profile.one_liner: TradingAgents-CN — 基于多智能体LLM的金融交易框架，旨在为中文开发者提供构建交易Agent的工具集，目前处于开源社区探索阶段。
full_profile.overview_zh: TradingAgents-CN 是一个开源的金融交易Agent框架，主要针对中文语境下的金融数据处理与交易逻辑构建。该项目通过多智能体协作模式，试图解决LLM在金融决策中的幻觉与逻辑推理问题。产品核心机制包括：定义不同角色的Agent（如分析师、策略师、执行者），通过LLM进行市场情绪分析与技术指标解读，并尝试将决策转化为可执行的交易指令。目前项目主要作为开发者工具存在，尚未形成闭环的交易系统，主要服务于量化交易爱好者及AI开发者。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 目前该项目为纯开源社区项目，暂无明确的商业化路径或变现计划。其商业逻辑处于缺失状态，主要依赖开源贡献者的热情维护。若未来转向商业化，可能采取API授权、企业级定制化部署或提供高阶金融数据接口订阅等模式，但目前缺乏付费用户验证及明确的定价逻辑。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone视角：金融交易Agent是AI领域的高价值赛道，但该项目目前仅处于“框架雏形”阶段。金融交易对确定性、延迟和合规性要求极高，单纯的LLM多智能体框架在缺乏私有数据飞轮和深度金融工程整合的情况下，难以产生Alpha。市场目前低估了“交易执行层”与“模型决策层”之间的鸿沟。风险在于该项目缺乏真实交易环境的验证，且金融合规性风险巨大。我们的投资论文倾向于寻找已在特定细分市场（如高频交易或特定资产类别）跑通闭环的团队，而非通用的开源框架。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native得16分，因为其尝试引入多智能体协作，但核心流程仍依赖通用模型，缺乏针对金融场景的深度重构；Niche得12分，金融交易虽是高价值场景，但该框架目前同质化严重；商业模式得5分，开源无变现计划；团队得8分，信息严重不足；加分项在于其在GitHub上获得了一定的社区关注（forks较多），减分项在于缺乏核心商业数据、创始人背景不明且存在信息严重不足的风险扣分。

title: Halluverse-M^3: A multitask multilingual benchmark for hallucination in LLMs
description_en: Hallucinations in large language models remain a persistent challenge, particularly in multilingual and generative settings where factual consistency is difficult to maintain. While recent models show strong performance on English-centric benchmarks, their behavior across languages, tasks, and hallucination types is not yet well understood. In this work, we introduce Halluverse-M^3, a dataset designed to enable systematic analysis of hallucinations across multiple languages, multiple generation tasks, and multiple hallucination categories. Halluverse-M^3 covers four languages, English, Arabic, Hindi, and Turkish, and supports two generation tasks: question answering and dialogue summarization. The dataset explicitly distinguishes between entity-level, relation-level, and sentence-level hallucinations. Hallucinated outputs are constructed through a controlled editing process and validated by human annotators, ensuring clear alignment between original content and hallucinated generations. Using this dataset, we evaluate a diverse set of contemporary open-source and proprietary language models on fine-grained hallucination detection. Our results show that question answering is consistently easier than dialogue summarization, while sentence-level hallucinations remain challenging even for the strongest models. Performance is highest in English and degrades in lower-resource languages, with Hindi exhibiting the lowest detection accuracy. Overall, Halluverse-M^3 provides a realistic and challenging benchmark for studying hallucinations in multilingual, multi-task settings. We release the dataset to support future research on hallucination detection and mitigation\footnote{https://huggingface.co/datasets/sabdalja/HalluVerse-M3}.
description_zh: Halluverse-M^3 是一个专门用于评估大语言模型（LLM）幻觉问题的多语言基准数据集。它涵盖了英语、阿拉伯语、印地语和土耳其语，支持问答和对话摘要两种核心任务。该数据集的核心创新在于将幻觉细分为实体级、关系级和句子级，并通过受控编辑和人工标注确保了幻觉样本的质量。该研究通过对多种开源及闭源模型进行基准测试，揭示了模型在不同语言和任务下的幻觉表现差异，为后续提升模型事实一致性提供了量化评估工具。

title: Improving Credit Card Fraud Detection with an Optimized Explainable Boosting Machine
description_en: Addressing class imbalance is a central challenge in credit card fraud detection, as it directly impacts predictive reliability in real-world financial systems. To overcome this, the study proposes an enhanced workflow based on the Explainable Boosting Machine (EBM)-a transparent, state-of-the-art implementation of the GA2M algorithm-optimized through systematic hyperparameter tuning, feature selection, and preprocessing refinement. Rather than relying on conventional sampling techniques that may introduce bias or cause information loss, the optimized EBM achieves an effective balance between accuracy and interpretability, enabling precise detection of fraudulent transactions while providing actionable insights into feature importance and interaction effects. Furthermore, the Taguchi method is employed to optimize both the sequence of data scalers and model hyperparameters, ensuring robust, reproducible, and systematically validated performance improvements. Experimental evaluation on benchmark credit card data yields an ROC-AUC of 0.983, surpassing prior EBM baselines (0.975) and outperforming Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, and Decision Tree models. These results highlight the potential of interpretable machine learning and data-driven optimization for advancing trustworthy fraud analytics in financial systems.
description_zh: 该研究针对信用卡欺诈检测中严重的类别不平衡问题，提出了一种基于可解释增强机（EBM）的优化工作流。不同于传统的采样技术，该方案通过系统性的超参数调优、特征选择及预处理优化，在保持模型高可解释性的同时提升了检测精度。核心创新在于引入Taguchi方法对数据缩放序列和模型超参数进行联合优化，确保了模型在基准数据集上达到0.983的ROC-AUC，优于传统的逻辑回归、随机森林及XGBoost模型。该研究旨在为金融风控领域提供一种既透明又高效的决策支持工具。

full_profile.overview_zh: 该研究针对信用卡欺诈检测中严重的类别不平衡问题，提出了一种基于可解释增强机（EBM）的优化工作流。不同于传统的采样技术，该方案通过系统性的超参数调优、特征选择及预处理优化，在保持模型高可解释性的同时提升了检测精度。核心创新在于引入Taguchi方法对数据缩放序列和模型超参数进行联合优化，确保了模型在基准数据集上达到0.983的ROC-AUC，优于传统的逻辑回归、随机森林及XGBoost模型。该研究旨在为金融风控领域提供一种既透明又高效的决策支持工具。

title: Parameter-free Dynamic Regret: Time-varying Movement Costs, Delayed Feedback, and Memory
description_en: In this paper, we study dynamic regret in unconstrained online convex optimization (OCO) with movement costs. Specifically, we generalize the standard setting by allowing the movement cost coefficients $λ_t$ to vary arbitrarily over time. Our main contribution is a novel algorithm that establishes the first comparator-adaptive dynamic regret bound for this setting, guaranteeing $\widetilde{\mathcal{O}}(\sqrt{(1+P_T)(T+\sum_t λ_t)})$ regret, where $P_T$ is the path length of the comparator sequence over $T$ rounds. This recovers the optimal guarantees for both static and dynamic regret in standard OCO as a special case where $λ_t=0$ for all rounds. To demonstrate the versatility of our results, we consider two applications: OCO with delayed feedback and OCO with time-varying memory. We show that both problems can be translated into time-varying movement costs, establishing a novel reduction specifically for the delayed feedback setting that is of independent interest. A crucial observation is that the first-order dependence on movement costs in our regret bound plays a key role in enabling optimal comparator-adaptive dynamic regret guarantees in both settings.
description_zh: 该论文研究了无约束在线凸优化（OCO）中的动态遗憾（Dynamic Regret）问题。核心贡献在于提出了一种新的算法，能够处理随时间任意变化的移动成本系数（λ_t）。该算法实现了首个比较器自适应（comparator-adaptive）的动态遗憾界，其复杂度为O~(sqrt((1+P_T)(T+sum λ_t)))。此外，作者将该框架应用于延迟反馈和时变记忆问题，通过将这些问题转化为时变移动成本模型，证明了该算法的通用性。这是一项纯数学与算法层面的理论突破，旨在优化在线学习系统在复杂动态环境下的决策效率。

title: Zero-shot Generalizable Graph Anomaly Detection with Mixture of Riemannian Experts
description_en: Graph Anomaly Detection (GAD) aims to identify irregular patterns in graph data, and recent works have explored zero-shot generalist GAD to enable generalization to unseen graph datasets. However, existing zero-shot GAD methods largely ignore intrinsic geometric differences across diverse anomaly patterns, substantially limiting their cross-domain generalization. In this work, we reveal that anomaly detectability is highly dependent on the underlying geometric properties and that embedding graphs from different domains into a single static curvature space can distort the structural signatures of anomalies. To address the challenge that a single curvature space cannot capture geometry-dependent graph anomaly patterns, we propose GAD-MoRE, a novel framework for zero-shot Generalizable Graph Anomaly Detection with a Mixture of Riemannian Experts architecture. Specifically, to ensure that each anomaly pattern is modeled in the Riemannian space where it is most detectable, GAD-MoRE employs a set of specialized Riemannian expert networks, each operating in a distinct curvature space. To align raw node features with curvature-specific anomaly characteristics, we introduce an anomaly-aware multi-curvature feature alignment module that projects inputs into parallel Riemannian spaces, enabling the capture of diverse geometric characteristics. Finally, to facilitate better generalization beyond seen patterns, we design a memory-based dynamic router that adaptively assigns each input to the most compatible expert based on historical reconstruction performance on similar anomalies. Extensive experiments in the zero-shot setting demonstrate that GAD-MoRE significantly outperforms state-of-the-art generalist GAD baselines, and even surpasses strong competitors that are few-shot fine-tuned with labeled data from the target domain.
description_zh: GAD-MoRE 针对图异常检测（GAD）领域中跨域泛化能力弱的问题，提出了一种创新的“黎曼专家混合”（Mixture of Riemannian Experts）架构。该研究的核心洞察在于：不同类型的异常模式依赖于不同的几何结构，而传统的单一曲率空间嵌入会扭曲异常特征。GAD-MoRE 通过部署多个在不同曲率空间运行的专家网络，使模型能够根据异常模式的几何特性自动选择最优的黎曼空间进行建模。该方法旨在实现无需针对特定数据集进行微调的零样本泛化，从而提升在复杂、异构图数据中的异常识别精度。

title: Designing a Robust, Bounded, and Smooth Loss Function for Improved Supervised Learning
description_en: The loss function is crucial to machine learning, especially in supervised learning frameworks. It is a fundamental component that controls the behavior and general efficacy of learning algorithms. However, despite their widespread use, traditional loss functions have significant drawbacks when dealing with high-dimensional and outlier-sensitive datasets, which frequently results in reduced performance and slower convergence during training. In this work, we develop a robust, bounded, and smooth (RoBoS-NN) loss function to resolve the aforementioned hindrances. The generalization ability of the loss function has also been theoretically analyzed to rigorously justify its robustness. Moreover, we implement RoboS-NN loss in the framework of a neural network (NN) to forecast time series and present a new robust algorithm named $\mathcal{L}_{\text{RoBoS}}$-NN. To assess the potential of $\mathcal{L}_{\text{RoBoS}}$-NN, we conduct experiments on multiple real-world datasets. In addition, we infuse outliers into data sets to evaluate the performance of $\mathcal{L}_{\text{RoBoS}}$-NN in more challenging scenarios. Numerical results show that $\mathcal{L}_{\text{RoBoS}}$-NN outperforms the other benchmark models in terms of accuracy measures.

title: Dreamful
description_en: Dreamful is a dream app I built to understand dreams as reflections of the subconscious, not predictions. You describe your dreams in your own words, and an AI model helps analyze the symbols, emotions, and patterns behind them. Instead of generic dream dictionaries, Dreamful offers personal and culturally aware insights shaped by psychology and daily life. Over time, it becomes a quiet place to notice recurring themes and better understand what is going on inside.

full_profile.overview_zh: Dreamful 是一款专注于梦境记录与分析的个人生活应用。用户通过自然语言描述梦境，AI 模型会对梦境中的符号、情绪及叙事模式进行深度解析，而非简单的词典式查询。该产品旨在帮助用户建立长期的梦境档案，通过识别重复出现的意象和心理模式，辅助用户进行自我觉察与心理梳理。目前产品处于极早期阶段，主要依赖用户的主动输入，缺乏复杂的多模态交互或主动式干预机制，产品形态较为单一。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 目前商业模式尚不明确，推测可能采取订阅制（Freemium）或单次分析付费。由于梦境分析属于低频且非刚需的心理健康辅助工具，用户付费意愿通常较低。与竞品相比，该产品缺乏差异化的心理学专业背书或临床数据支持，难以建立高价值的商业闭环。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone 视角：梦境分析是一个极度碎片化且难以规模化的利基市场。虽然情感陪伴类应用（如 Character.AI）表现强劲，但 Dreamful 仅停留在“工具”层面，缺乏社交属性或深度心理治疗的专业性。该方向的 Alpha 信号微弱，市场极易被通用大模型（如 ChatGPT 的自定义 GPTs）覆盖。风险在于用户留存率极低，且缺乏数据飞轮。我们的投资论文倾向于寻找具有高频交互、强用户粘性或具备特定行业壁垒的 AI Native 产品，Dreamful 目前尚不符合标准。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native 得 12 分，因为其核心仅是基于 LLM 的文本分析，缺乏 Agent 规划或主动交互能力；Niche 得 8 分，梦境分析属于心理健康细分，但同质化严重；商业模式得 7 分，缺乏明确的变现路径；团队得 6 分，因缺乏公开背景信息，无法评估其专业性；减分项包括信息严重不足（-3）及创始人背景不明（-2）。总分 38 分，属于不感兴趣的范畴，缺乏 Alpha 潜力。
full_profile.metrics_summary: Product Hunt 票数：12.0票；其他数据暂无公开数据。

full_profile.one_liner: ANORA — 面向生成式工作流的创意开发环境，旨在为开发者提供AI驱动的构建空间，创始人背景及具体技术细节暂缺。
full_profile.overview_zh: ANORA 定位为生成式工作流的创意环境（Creative Environment），旨在解决开发者在构建复杂AI Agent或生成式应用时，缺乏统一、高效的开发与调试空间的问题。产品试图将AI模型调用、工作流编排与创意实验整合在一个界面中。然而，从目前公开的有限信息来看，该产品尚处于早期阶段，缺乏明确的差异化技术路径，也未展示出成熟的Agent编排能力或闭环数据飞轮，其核心功能与现有的低代码Agent平台（如Dify）或IDE插件相比，缺乏显著的竞争优势。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 目前该产品未披露明确的商业模式。参考同类工具，预计未来可能采取SaaS订阅制或基于API调用的用量付费模式。由于缺乏用户规模和差异化价值主张，其商业逻辑尚处于验证阶段，目前无法判断其是否具备长期付费意愿的支撑能力。
full_profile.insight_zh: Creekstone视角：生成式工作流开发环境是一个极度拥挤的赛道，Cursor、LangChain、Dify等巨头已占据开发者心智。ANORA若想获得Alpha，必须在特定垂直领域（如多模态创意生成或特定行业工作流）实现深度集成，而非仅仅做一个通用的创意环境。目前市场对这类工具的估值逻辑已从“工具属性”转向“Agent闭环能力”，ANORA缺乏技术壁垒，面临极高的替代风险。

title: Are You Happy?
description_en: Are you happy? That’s the only question. You tap Yes or No. No forms, no goals, no guilt. Just one moment to check in with yourself. The app is different every time you open it: new colors, fonts, and little details. Same question, same one tap. It’s built to feel light and a bit surprising.
description_zh: Are You Happy? 是一款极度精简的情绪追踪工具。其核心机制非常简单：用户打开应用后，系统仅询问“你快乐吗？”，用户通过“是”或“否”进行单次点击反馈。产品设计强调无负担感，摒弃了复杂的表单、目标设定和数据分析功能。每次打开应用时，界面颜色、字体和细节会发生随机变化，旨在提供一种轻量级、带有惊喜感的自我觉察体验。目前该产品在市场上的表现非常有限，缺乏深度的数据沉淀或AI驱动的个性化反馈机制。
track_reason: 面向C端用户，提供情绪记录和反馈。

score_breakdown.reason: 产品功能过于简单，缺乏AI核心能力，且市场表现极差，属于典型的无壁垒工具，不具备投资价值。
plus: 设计风格具有一定的轻量化美感
极简交互降低了用户使用门槛
minus: 缺乏AI技术支撑
用户粘性极低
缺乏明确的商业化路径
Product Hunt数据极差，市场验证不足

full_profile.one_liner: Are You Happy? — 一款极简主义情绪打卡应用，通过每日“是/否”询问记录用户状态，缺乏AI深度集成，创始人背景信息缺失。
full_profile.overview_zh: Are You Happy? 是一款极度精简的情绪追踪工具。其核心机制非常简单：用户打开应用后，系统仅询问“你快乐吗？”，用户通过“是”或“否”进行单次点击反馈。产品设计强调无负担感，摒弃了复杂的表单、目标设定和数据分析功能。每次打开应用时，界面颜色、字体和细节会发生随机变化，旨在提供一种轻量级、带有惊喜感的自我觉察体验。目前该产品在市场上的表现非常有限，缺乏深度的数据沉淀或AI驱动的个性化反馈机制。
full_profile.biz_model_zh: 目前该产品未见明确的商业化路径。作为一款极简工具，其潜在的商业模式可能包括订阅制（解锁高级视觉主题或长期统计报告）或Freemium模式。然而，由于产品功能过于单一且缺乏高价值的反馈循环，用户付费意愿极低，难以支撑持续的商业增长，与Character.AI等具备高粘性的竞品相比，商业逻辑极其薄弱。
full_profile.insight_zh: 从Creekstone的投资视角来看，该项目属于典型的“微型工具”，而非具备规模化潜力的AI产品。虽然“极简主义”在心理健康领域有一定市场，但该产品缺乏AI Native的核心能力，如情绪趋势分析、主动式心理干预或个性化陪伴。市场目前更倾向于具备Agent能力的陪伴型AI，而非简单的记录器。该项目的风险在于极高的同质化和极低的进入门槛，缺乏护城河，难以在竞争激烈的Lifestyle赛道中留存用户。
full_profile.score_narrative_zh: AI Native得5分，因为产品仅为简单的UI交互，无任何AI模型或Agent逻辑；Niche得8分，因为情绪记录赛道竞争激烈，该产品缺乏差异化壁垒；商业模式得5分，因为缺乏清晰的变现计划；团队得5分，因为缺乏公开的创始人背景信息；加减分项中，因Product Hunt票数极低（7票）且缺乏核心技术壁垒，扣除15分。整体分数较低，反映了该项目在当前AI浪潮下缺乏投资价值。
full_profile.metrics_summary: Product Hunt票数：7票；GitHub stars：暂无公开数据；用户数：暂无公开数据；ARR：暂无公开数据。
